Petition For Authorization To Pay Litigation Counsel Fees
Off Calendar Mar-22-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Daniel Conrad for Thomas Lucas (Conservator and Petitioner)

Mr. Conrad responds to the examiners notes as follows:

1. Attorney conferences have been held twice within two years and that the two associates spent 8/10ths of an hour at $45.00 per hour; if this is improper it can be deducted.
2. In terms of research, 10 hours were billed, 4 hours were unbilled, they do not hide the fact that they do research, that there were complicated issues, the Mr. Lucas had to file suit against an attorney, that there were bankruptcy issues regarding the defendant involved, that it was all itemized and that all of the research time is appropriate.
3. The costs fees were all related to this case, stating that the petition filing fee was $575.00 and various response and subsequent filing fees.
4. The messenger charge totaled $19.00 and it was for a document response delivered to Defendants attorney.

The Court went over the costs issues and requested that counsel submit an itemization of costs and the internal conferences will be taken under submission. Itemization of costs shall be filed by March 16, 2016 and the Petition for Authorization to Pay Litigation Counsel Fees will be under submission as of that date.

Petition For Order Bring Trusts Under Continuing Court Supervision; For Order Revoking Powers Of Tr.
Off Calendar Apr-08-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lawrence Siracusa for Thomas Lucas (Petitioner). Edward Koplowitz for and with Elaine Andrews. John Minton for Shirley Andrews. Ernest Der Court appointed Counsel for Rosemary Maisel.

The Court stated that it appears that Shirley is not pursuing the roll of Trustee. Mr. Siracusa stated that he just filed a declination to serve from Frederick Maisel, Jr. and from Bernard McAuley (endorsed copies provided), that the Amended Trust documents have been received, that the Beneficiary of Rosemary's Trust is the Salvation Army and that there are problems with the amendment of the Survivor's Trust. Mr. Siracusa also stated that Frederick's Trust did not give Rosemary power to amend the Survivor's Trust, that there are issues with the Qualified Resident's Trust and the share in the Co-op, and that they will have to file a petition for authority to revoke the Trusts. Mr. Koplowitz stated that his Client is concerned with the allegations in the petition, spoke of time spent on services performed, and stated that bills have been provided in detail and that documents have also been provided. Mr. Siracusa stated that everything was transferred as far as he knows but that they have not received bank statements for the period prior to appointment of his Client. Mr. Der spoke of his Client's medical condition and stated that his Client agrees to having a Neutral Fiduciary in charge. Mr. Minton stated that Shirley wishes to resign for personal reasons and that she denies the allegations. The Court granted the Petition for Order to Bring Trusts Under Continuing Court Supervision, etc. appointing Thomas Lucas as Successor Trustee (subject to review of the order). Bond in Frederick's Trust is set at $616,000.00 and bond in Rosemary's Trust is set at $74,000.00. Mr. Der is to remain on in the Trust until all of the issues are sorted out. Mr. Siracusa submitted a revised order.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Loos.
